calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-19-2026 Set For Trial On Sep-28-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Feb-19-2026 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DARIO MENDEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS.

Plaintiff Dario Mendez moves to quash deposition subpoenas for the production of business records issued to MedicWest Ambulance, San Francisco Emergency Medical Association, and Health Diagnostic of CA, on grounds of privacy and overbreadth. The motion is denied, and the court orders Plaintiff to pay $480 in sanctions to Defendant by March 19, 2026.

Plaintiff has put at issue the medical conditions he claims were caused by the accident in this case, and has thereby waived his privacy and physician-patient confidentiality rights as to those conditions. (Vinson v. Superior Court (1987) 43 Cal.3d 833, 842.) Defendant presents evidence that the subpoenas were issued to those treaters Plaintiff identified as treating him after the accident. Plaintiff's claims of injury are broad, and the subpoenas seek records only from the prior 10 yeras. Plaintiff raises the issue that psychiatric records may be part of the records requested, but offers no evidentiary support for that assertion, and the court therefore need not decide whether Plaintiff has put his mental health records at issue with his claim that he suffered shock and injury to his nervous system or great mental pain and suffering.

The court concludes that Plaintiff's motion lacked substantial justification and orders Plaintiff to pay $480 in sanctions to Defendant.

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Feb-19-2026 Off Calendar
